description:
A SINGLE UNINTERRUPTED PANORAMIC PAINTING of one continuous antique dealer's cabinet interior, rendered as ONE flowing seamless mural with absolutely NO vertical lines dividing the image, NO triptych frames, NO panels, NO image seams, NO black borders between any parts — strictly forbidden to split the image into thirds or sections; the result must read as a single oil-painted scroll-like view of the same room.

Architecture (described continuously across the width): the viewer looks into a cozy three-walled interior. At the far LEFT of the image (roughly 0–18% from the left edge), a LEFT SIDE WALL angles forward toward the viewer. Around 18–80% (the central majority of the image) we see the FLAT BACK WALL straight on. From around 80% to the right edge, a RIGHT SIDE WALL angles forward toward the viewer. The walls meet at TWO natural interior CORNERS (left corner around 18%, right corner around 80%) — visible only as a soft change of wall plane and gentle painted shading at the join, never as a hard black line. ONE continuous wooden parquet floor runs unbroken across the bottom from edge to edge. ONE continuous ceiling moulding runs unbroken across the top from edge to edge. Vintage damask wallpaper covers the entire continuous wall surface from left edge to right edge.

Feature details (named by horizontal position from left = 0% to right = 100% of the panorama):
• At ~5%: the LEFT SIDE WALL holds a TALL BUILT-IN BOOKSHELF running floor-to-ceiling, packed with old leather-bound books and a brass bookshelf rail.
• At ~12%: a tall WOODEN-FRAMED WINDOW is set HIGH in the LEFT SIDE WALL (just above the desk lamp height). Warm daylight streams through it. The window glass MUST be FULLY TRANSPARENT alpha channel (no painted sky inside the glass).
• Strong FOREGROUND ANCHOR from 0–22%: a MAHOGANY WRITING DESK juts forward from the LEFT-BOTTOM CORNER into the viewer's space, the front edge cropping out of frame at the left. We see the near front corner of the desk and the top surface from a slight top-down angle. On the desk: a few scattered sheets of parchment, a brass inkwell with a quill pen, a small green-shade Art Déco desk lamp lit warmly, a leather-cornered blotter pad. Leave bare areas on the desk surface for additional small objects to be composited later.
• At ~50% (dead center, on the BACK WALL): a heavy wooden FRONT DOOR with a polished brass letterbox slot at mid-height, set flush into the back wall (no protruding frame). A thin Persian runner on the floor leads up to the door.
• At ~50% on the CEILING: an ART DÉCO BRASS PENDANT CEILING LAMP (plafonnier) hangs down — a multi-tiered chandelier with frosted glass tulip shades casting a warm pool of light down toward the door.
• At ~88%: the RIGHT SIDE WALL holds a STONE FIREPLACE (small warm fire burning in the hearth, carved stone mantelpiece with a few decorative trinkets, chimney column rising to the ceiling).
• At ~95%: a tall WOODEN-FRAMED WINDOW is set HIGH in the RIGHT SIDE WALL beside the fireplace. The window glass MUST be FULLY TRANSPARENT alpha channel (no painted sky inside the glass).
• Foreground from ~78–100%: a large Persian rug on the parquet floor, a small wooden side table, and a clear empty floor space (no objects) reserved for an armchair that will be composited later.

Lighting: soft warm directional light from the upper-left, consistent across the whole panorama (windows + desk lamp + pendant lamp + fireplace all reinforce a cozy lit interior). Cohesive Art Déco rendering throughout: cream parchment paper texture, sepia ink line-art, soft forest green wash, brass and warm wood tones, subtle paper grain. No people, no text, no captions, no signatures, no watermark.

CRITICAL FINAL CONSTRAINT — the only transparency in the output PNG is on the TWO window glass areas (one on the left side wall, one on the right side wall). EVERY OTHER pixel is opaque. Absolutely no vertical seams, no black bars, no panel borders, no thirds dividers anywhere in the image.

A wide impressionistic exterior view of a sunlit Parisian streetscape at midday, intended to be placed BEHIND the reference cabinet image — visible only through the two transparent window apertures of the reference (one HIGH on the LEFT SIDE WALL of the left third, one HIGH on the RIGHT SIDE WALL of the right third). Compose horizontally so that: the LEFT portion (aligned with the left third of the reference where the left-side-wall window sits) reveals leafy chestnut treetops swaying gently against a warm pastel sky; the CENTER portion of this exterior image is visually neutral (it will be hidden behind the opaque back wall of the reference); the RIGHT portion (aligned with the right third of the reference where the right-side-wall window sits) reveals classic Parisian Haussmannian roof slopes, slate-grey rooftops, a distant chimney pot or two. Soft impressionistic brushstrokes, warm midday daylight, soft pastel sky tones (cream, peach, dusty blue), no people, no text. Same aspect ratio as the reference image (16:9) so they overlay exactly.

A heavy wooden front door, closed, with a polished brass letterbox slot at mid-height. The door is intended to be composited on top of the CENTER zone of the reference image (the entrance wall), at the exact same position, perspective and scale as the door already shown in the reference. Match the reference's eye-level perspective, the soft warm light from upper-left, the rendering style (Art Déco line-art + sepia/forest green wash on cream parchment), and the door's apparent height in the reference. Isolated subject only — door panel, frame and letterbox visible — on transparent background, nothing else.

A vintage tufted Chesterfield armchair in deep emerald-green velvet, three-quarter front view facing slightly toward the viewer. The armchair is intended to be placed on the Persian rug in the RIGHT zone (reading nook) of the reference image, in the empty floor space between the side table and the fireplace. Match the reference's eye-level perspective, scale (the armchair should fit naturally on the rug without overlapping the fireplace), and lighting (soft warm light from upper-left). Use the same Art Déco line-art + sepia/forest green wash style on cream parchment. Isolated subject on transparent background — armchair only, no rug, no nook elements.

A rolled-up vintage newspaper tied with a string, lying flat on a desk surface. The newspaper is intended to be placed on the mahogany writing desk in the LEFT zone of the reference image, viewed from the same slight top-down angle the desk surface has. Match the reference's perspective, scale (the rolled newspaper should look like a regular ~40cm log on the desk), lighting from upper-left, and the same Art Déco line-art + sepia/forest green wash style. Isolated subject on transparent background — newspaper only, no desk.

A small leather-bound notebook, closed, brown cover with gilded edges, lying flat next to the rolled newspaper on the same writing desk. The notebook is intended to be placed on the mahogany writing desk in the LEFT zone of the reference image. Match the reference's perspective (same slight top-down angle as the desk), scale (notebook ~A6 size on the desk), lighting from upper-left, and the same Art Déco rendering style. Isolated subject on transparent background — notebook only, no desk.

A vintage gramophone with brass horn on a small wooden side table. Intended to be placed next to the armchair in the RIGHT zone (reading nook) of the reference image. Match the reference's eye-level perspective, scale (the gramophone + side table should reach about the height of the armchair's backrest), lighting from upper-left, and the same Art Déco line-art + sepia/forest green wash on cream parchment. Isolated subject on transparent background — gramophone and its small side table only.

A single closed white envelope as if dropped on the floor, slightly rotated, soft shadow underneath. Intended to be placed on the floor right in front of the door in the CENTER zone of the reference image. Match the reference's perspective on the floor (slight top-down) and lighting from upper-left. Same Art Déco rendering style. Isolated subject on transparent background — envelope only.
